select_when: Work is being delegated across an objective gap where the executing party may benefit from choices that do not serve the owner of the outcome.
Specialized knowledge, hidden intentions, or role ambiguity make it hard to tell whether the agent's behavior is aligned with the principal's real goals.
The main challenge is to design incentives, oversight, and role clarity so delegated work stays tied to the larger objective.
Dashboards or formal compliance are starting to look like substitutes for actual alignment, so the team needs to inspect divergence underneath the metrics.
avoid_when: The response is collapsing into micromanagement of the current case instead of diagnosing the machine-level design that created the drift.
The model is being used to assume bad faith when the main issue is capability, capacity, or a muddled objective rather than a true alignment split.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model focused on delegated-objective divergence, hidden behavior, and system design for alignment rather than broad management advice.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor multi-agent architecture sprawl because the raw source is stronger on alignment gaps, role clarity, and machine-level diagnosis.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that spans organizations, internal cognition analogies, consulting, and AI-agent design.

select_when.note: The raw source explicitly names delegated objective gaps, information asymmetry, incentive alignment, and metric-hidden divergence.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are grounded in the source warnings about micromanagement and confusing misalignment with other causes of poor outcomes.

input_type.note: The source activates around delegation, alignment gaps, and hidden behavior; the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The source naturally yields system redesign around incentives and accountability, though the typed output label is a compact reviewed artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Principal-agent reasoning is explicitly structural and causal about alignment and hidden action, while the taxonomy labels remain normalized.
